name: Trilho Azul — circular route Parque Florestal de Monsanto
name_original: Trilho Azul — percurso circular Parque Florestal de Monsanto
region_intro: Hiking around Campolide offers direct access to the expansive Monsanto Forest Park, Lisbon's largest green space, often referred to as the "Lungs of Lisbon." This protected forested area features an extensive network of trails through dense woods and rolling hills. Hikers can explore diverse landscapes, including ancient limestone quarries and areas replanted with varied flora. The region also provides panoramic views of Lisbon, the Tagus River, and the Atlantic Ocean from various viewpoints within the park. Best hiking trails around Campolide The most popular hiking route is Trilho Azul — circular route Parque Florestal de Monsanto, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the diverse forest landscapes of Monsanto Forest Park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Panorâmico de Monsanto – Altice Tower loop from Campolide, a difficult 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. This trail offers significant elevation gain and panoramic views from the Altice Tower area. Local hikers also love the Campolide loop from São Sebastião da Pedreira, a 2.5 miles (4.0 km) trail leading through urban green spaces and local streets, often completed in about 1 hour 7 minutes. Hiking around Campolide is defined by dense woods, rolling hills, and historical features like old quarries and the Aqueduto das Águas Livres. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging hikes. The routes in Campolide are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 10,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Campolide's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Campolide?
A: Hiking around Campolide primarily involves trails within the vast Monsanto Forest Park. You'll find diverse landscapes, including dense woods, rolling hills, and areas replanted with varied flora. Some routes, like the "Trail of the Six Quarries," even pass through ancient limestone quarries. The trails range from easy walks to more challenging hikes with significant elevation gain, offering a mix of natural paths and some paved sections.

Q: How many hiking trails are available around Campolide?
A: There are over 19 hiking routes available around Campolide, with options for various ability levels. The komoot community has highly rated these trails, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Campolide?
A: Lisbon generally enjoys a mild climate, making hiking pleasant for much of the year. Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) are ideal, with comfortable temperatures and lush greenery. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter offers cooler, often sunny days, perfect for exploring the trails without the heat.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes near Campolide?
A: Yes, Campolide offers several challenging hikes, particularly within Monsanto Forest Park. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23659540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panorâmico de Monsanto – Altice Tower loop from Campolide&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 5.3-mile (8.5 km) path that features significant elevation gain and offers panoramic views from the Altice Tower area.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Campolide suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, many trails in Monsanto Forest Park are suitable for families. The park offers easy walks and green spaces like Alto de Serafina park, which has play areas. The extensive network of trails allows for shorter, less strenuous options perfect for younger hikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380910513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trilho Azul — circular route Parque Florestal de Monsanto&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.3-mile (6.9 km) route that explores diverse forest landscapes, suitable for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails near Campolide?
A: Monsanto Forest Park is generally dog-friendly, allowing pets on leash. It's a popular spot for locals to walk their dogs. Always ensure your dog is under control and that you clean up after them to maintain the park's natural beauty for everyone.

Q: Are there many circular hiking routes available in Campolide?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option in the Campolide area, especially within Monsanto Forest Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380910513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trilho Azul — circular route Parque Florestal de Monsanto&lt;/a&gt; is a well-known example. The "Percurso Volta do Planalto" is another perimeter trail within Monsanto, approximately 7 km long, offering diverse forest landscapes and views.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking near Campolide?
A: While hiking near Campolide, you'll primarily explore Monsanto Forest Park, which features dense woods, rolling hills, and unique natural elements like ancient limestone quarries. The park also offers breathtaking panoramic views of Lisbon, the Tagus River, and even the Atlantic Ocean from various viewpoints, including the Monsanto Panoramic Viewpoint. The historic Águas Livres Aqueduct is also a prominent landmark in the Campolide district itself.

Q: What other attractions are close to the hiking areas in Campolide?
A: Beyond the trails, Campolide offers access to several notable attractions. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32224832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Águas Livres Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt;, a remarkable piece of 18th-century engineering. Nearby, you can also find the beautifully landscaped Parque Eduardo VII, which provides stunning views of Lisbon, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8178196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eduardo VII Park Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. For a broader view of Lisbon, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8190638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from the Rua Augusta Arch&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1000280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Luzia viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, both easily accessible from Campolide.

Q: How can I get to the hiking trails in Monsanto Forest Park from Campolide?
A: Monsanto Forest Park is easily accessible from Campolide and the city center via car or bus. A dedicated track, the Corredor Verde de Monsanto (Green Corridor of Monsanto), also allows for walking, jogging, or cycling directly from Parque Eduardo VII to the forest, providing a pleasant way to reach the trails.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in Monsanto Forest Park?
A: Yes, parking is generally available at various points around Monsanto Forest Park. Given its size and popularity, there are designated parking areas to accommodate visitors accessing different sections of the park. However, during peak times, it's advisable to arrive early.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails around Campolide?
A: Hikers consistently rate the trails around Campolide highly, with an average of 4.5 stars. They often praise the direct access to the expansive Monsanto Forest Park, the diverse forest landscapes, and the panoramic views of Lisbon and the Tagus River. The variety of routes, from easy walks to more challenging paths, is also a frequently mentioned positive aspect.

Q: Can I hike near the historic Águas Livres Aqueduct?
A: Yes, you can hike near the impressive Águas Livres Aqueduct. There are routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32224832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aqueduto das Águas Livres – Águas Livres Aqueduct loop from São Sebastião da Pedreira&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to explore this magnificent 18th-century structure and its surroundings. The aqueduct itself is a prominent landmark in Campolide.
tips:
Parque Eduardo VII
This famous park is named after British King Edward VII who visited the city in the twentieth century. Parque Eduardo VII is the largest park in the center of Lisbon. The park is built against a hill.
Temporarily closed (May 2024) and there doesn't seem to be much action there.

On the Planalto Trail in the Monsanto Forest Park in Lisbon, you can find a series of wooden sculptures that are part of a very special visual and natural experience.
The sculptures are located in the cypress forest area, part of the Percurso Volta do Planalto, a circular trail of approximately 7 km.
